data.title: Loblaw looks to invest $10B in Canada over next five years
data.description:
• Loblaw (TSX:L:CA) said it will invest $2.2 billion into the Canadian economy in 2025, as it looks to improve access to more affordable food and healthcare services for Canadians.
  • The firm also expects to create an anticipated 8,000 jobs in communities across the country.
  • Loblaw (TSX:L:CA) said it anticipates similar levels of investment over the next five years and expects to invest more than $10 billion by 2030.
  • The investment plan includes: 80 new stores under the No Frills, Maxi, Shoppers Drug Mart, Pharmaprix and T&T banners, including approximately 50 hard discount stores.
  • It also includes the renovation of more than 300 grocery and pharmacy locations, including adding approximately 100 new Shoppers Drug Mart pharmacy care clinics, and the development of its modernized supply chain.

data.title: Parsons misses Q4 top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Parsons press release (NYSE:PSN): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.78 misses by $0.13.
  • Revenue of $1.73B (+16.1% Y/Y) misses by $30M.
  • Organic revenue growth of 14%; eighth consecutive quarter of double-digit organic growth.
  • Record adjusted EBITDA of $147 million increases 14%.
  • Cash flow from operations of $127 million.
  • Record contract awards increase 34% year-over-year to $1.7 billion.
  • Establishing fiscal year 2025 guidance and reiterating long-term growth targets.

  • Fiscal Year 2025 Guidance

    The table below summarizes the company's fiscal year 2025 guidance.

    Fiscal Year2025 Guidance
    Revenue $7.0 billion - $7.5 billion vs. consensus of $7.34B
    Adjusted EBITDA including non-controlling interest $640 million - $710 million
    Cash Flow from Operating Activities $420 million - $480 million

    Net income guidance is not presented as the company believes volatility associated with interest, taxes, depreciation, amortization and other matters affecting net income, including but not limited to one-time and nonrecurring events and the impact of M&A, will preclude the company from providing, with reasonable certainty, net income guidance for fiscal year 2025.

    Reiterating Long-term Growth Targets

    The table below summarizes the company's long-term growth targets.

    Long-term Growth Targets Highlights
    Organic Revenue Growth Mid- single-digit or better organic growth Growth is off a revenue base that is $1.3 billion higher than fiscal year 2023
    Total Revenue Growth Mid- single-digit or better organic growth + M&A Growth is off a revenue base that is $1.3 billion higher than fiscal year 2023
    Adjusted EBITDA Margin Expansion Average 20 - 30 basis pointsper year Continual margin improvement opportunity. Adjusted EBITDA expansion also off a higher revenue base
    Free Cash Flow Conversion >100% Robust free cash flow generation to fund future organic and inorganic investment opportunities

data.title: FREYR Battery changes name to T1 Energy
data.description:
• FREYR Battery (NYSE:FREY) has renamed itself as T1 Energy in a strategic rebranding that also includes a new corporate logo, a fresh visual identity, and a forthcoming ticker symbol change to “TE” on the NYSE.
  • The T1 brand represents the company’s commitment to becoming a vertically integrated U.S. solar + battery storage leader from its new corporate headquarters in Austin, Texas, the company said in a news release.
  • In connection with the change, the company expects that effective as of the open of business on March 3, 2025, its common stock and warrants, which trade on the NYSE, will cease trading under the ticker symbols “FREY” and “FREY WS”, respectively, and commence trading under the ticker symbols “TE” and “TE WS”.
  • FREY +0.96% premarket to $2.09
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: RioCan Real Estate Investment Trust GAAP EPS of $0.42, revenue of $357.6M beats by $142.49M
data.description:
• RioCan Real Estate Investment Trust press release (OTCPK:RIOCF): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.42.
  • FFO Adjusted per unit - diluted $0.47.
  • Revenue of $357.6M (+20.4% Y/Y) beats by $142.49M.
  • High demand locations generate new leasing spreads of 36.7% for 2024; blended leasing spreads of 18.7%.
  • Record-breaking committed occupancy at 98.0%; retail committed occupancy at high water mark of 98.7%.
  • 98% completion of the 372 expected Fourth Quarter condominium and townhouse interim closings to date.
  • Adjusted Debt to Adjusted EBITDA improved to 8.98x from 9.28x at the end of the prior year.
  • Based on our FFO guidance for 2025, we expect to maintain a payout ratio within our long-term target range of 55%-65%:

    • Outlook 2025

    FFO per unit

    $1.89 to $1.92 vs. consensus of $1.87

    FFO Payout Ratio

    ~ 60%

    Commercial Same Property NOI growth

    ~3.5%

data.title: Canaan reports month-on-month increase in Bitcoin production in January
data.description:
• Canaan (NASDAQ:CAN) reported an increase in the Bitcoin (BTC-USD) mined in January, as compared to the prior month.
  • The firm said it had mined 88 BTC in January, as compared to 62 in December.
  • The firm's month-end operating hashrate stood at 5.53 EH/s, while its average all-in power cost came in at $0.04/kWh.
  • "In January, we made solid progress across all segments of our business as we expanded our customer base, announced two Avalon Home consumer products, and advanced our self-mining capacity, leading to an increase in our bitcoin holdings," said CEO, Nangeng Zhang.
  • The company remained on track to reach 10 EH/s in North America and 15 EH/s globally by mid-2025.
  • Shares were up 3.4% in premarket trade.

data.title: Fee focus: Vanguard's VOO takes the ETF crown from State Street's SPY
data.description:
Products tracking the S&P 500 (SP500) make up a good percentage of investor portfolios - and for good reason. The benchmark index just closed at a fresh record high and has returned more than 20% annually over the past two years. While there are many ways to gain exposure to the S&P 500 (SP500), many have been focusing on the cheapest ways to do so, especially with popular ETFs.

Case in point: The first-ever U.S.-listed ETF, the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSEARCA:SPY), was just topped by its arch-rival in terms of assets under management. The Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (NYSEARCA:VOO) had nearly $632B in AUM, compared to the $630B of SPY, according to calculations as of Tuesday. Not far behind is the iShares Core S&P 500 ETF (NYSEARCA:IVV), which has $609B of assets under management based on its share count.

What happened? Investors and financial advisors are getting more price-conscious, especially in the era of DIY investing. The low-cost Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (NYSEARCA:VOO) and BlackRock's (BLK) iShares Core S&P 500 ETF (NYSEARCA:IVV) feature an expense ratio of just 0.03%, compared with the 0.095% expense ratio State Street's (STT) SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSEARCA:SPY). The first two have also recorded inflows of $115B and $87B, respectively, in 2024, compared with the $17B in inflows for SPY. State Street (STT) does offer the SPDR Portfolio S&P 500 ETF (NYSEARCA:SPLG), with an expense ratio of just 0.02%, which is more catered to buy-and-hold investors and can also be helpful for those who have a smaller portfolio.

Why would one opt for SPY? Name-brand and founder status have been associated with the ETF, as well as better liquidity and spreads for trading and options. But times are indeed changing. The S&P 500 ETF crown now belongs to Vanguard, which has been cutting its fees across the board. Earlier this month, the investment company announced the "largest ever expense ratio reduction," which will impact 87 open-end mutual and exchange-traded funds and "save Vanguard's investors more than $350M in 2025 alone."

data.title: XP Inc. targets over 10% revenue growth in 2025 amid expansion in fixed-income and cross-sell initiatives
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: XP Inc. (NASDAQ:XP) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Thiago Maffra highlighted a record-setting year with significant achievements, including client assets reaching BRL1.22 trillion, a 9% year-over-year increase, and gross revenues of BRL18 billion, reflecting 15% growth. The company posted its highest quarterly adjusted net income of BRL1.2 billion in Q4 2024, contributing to a 17% growth for the year.
  • XP achieved a 28.7% ROTE with a 376 basis points expansion year-over-year and maintained a BIS ratio of 17.7% despite distributing BRL2 billion in dividends. Adjusted diluted EPS grew 16% year-over-year, supported by the share buyback program.
  • CFO Victor Mansur emphasized the company's strong performance in retail fixed-income and corporate issue services, attributing growth to enhanced distribution capabilities and market-making strategies. "Our total gross revenue posted 15% growth in 2024," Mansur stated.
  • The company reiterated its confidence in achieving 2026 guidance, with a target EBT margin range of 30% to 34% and a revenue CAGR of 12% to 22%.

Outlook

  • Management expects net new money to remain steady at BRL20 billion per quarter in retail, with a projected total revenue growth of over 10% in 2025. Fixed-income products and cross-sell initiatives, such as credit cards and insurance, are expected to drive this growth.
  • The company outlined plans to expand its proprietary tools and segmentation strategies, aiming to enhance client satisfaction and loyalty. New credit card offerings tailored for affluent and private banking clients are set for release in early Q2 2025.
  • XP sees potential in life insurance, with penetration currently at 2% compared to the market average of 17%. Management forecasts life insurance revenues to grow faster than the 41% rate achieved in 2024.

Financial Results

  • Gross revenues reached BRL18 billion in 2024, a 15% increase year-over-year. Retail revenue grew 14%, with fixed-income revenues up 49% for the year.
  • Corporate and issuer services marked a 45% growth for the year, driven by a 46% increase in issuer services revenues and a 45% rise in corporate revenues. The company's corporate securities book grew to BRL32 billion.
  • Adjusted net income for 2024 totaled BRL4.5 billion, with a 17% growth year-over-year. The adjusted EPS grew 16% year-over-year, supported by strict cost controls and operational efficiencies.
  • SG&A grew only 2% year-over-year in Q4, despite investments in new products and the hiring of 800 internal advisors.

Q&A

  • Thiago Batista, UBS: Questioned the 12% quarter-over-quarter increase in risk-weighted assets. CFO Victor Mansur explained that "credit spreads risk is allocated at market risk," reflecting a shift in regulatory accounting.
  • Eduardo Rosman, BTG: Asked about competition from banks on tax-exempt instruments. CEO Maffra noted, "We have developed ways, technology, and instruments to compete against the banks" and highlighted improved competitiveness in fixed-income products.
  • Guilherme Grespan, JPMorgan: Asked about mark-to-market impacts on equity. CFO Mansur clarified that the impact was from balance sheet hedges, which will be addressed by new regulations in 2025.
  • Gustavo Schroden, Citi: Queried about productivity improvements among IFAs. Maffra emphasized plans to replicate internal advisor tools and techniques for the IFA network in 2025.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed a slightly positive tone, focusing on capital allocation, competitive positioning, and growth potential in new verticals. Concerns about competition and market risks were evident but not dominant.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing robust growth, disciplined cost management, and strategic initiatives. Statements like "We are confident in our capital level" reflected assurance in meeting guidance.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management displayed consistent confidence, with a focus on steady execution toward 2026 targets.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language remained consistent, projecting over 10% revenue growth for 2025, aligned with 2026 goals. Analysts’ focus shifted slightly toward competitive pressures and strategic differentiation.
  • Key metrics such as gross revenues, net income, and ROTE showed sequential growth. Adjusted EPS improved, reflecting enhanced profitability from cost controls and operational leverage.
  • Management's tone remained assertive, reiterating confidence in its ecosystem's resilience, while analysts showed cautious optimism about competition and macroeconomic impacts.

Risks and Concerns

  • Analysts raised concerns about competitive pressures from incumbent banks and the potential impact of regulatory changes on tax-exempt instruments.
  • Management highlighted mitigation strategies, including enhanced competitiveness in fixed-income products, expansion of proprietary tools for advisors, and diversification of revenue streams.
  • Seasonal weakness in DCM activity for Q1 2025 was noted, but management expects this to be offset by secondary market activity.

Final Takeaway

XP Inc. delivered a record-breaking year with robust growth across multiple verticals, supported by strong performance in retail fixed-income and corporate services. The company remains confident in its ability to achieve 2026 guidance, driven by strategic initiatives in cross-sell products, segmentation, and technology enhancements. Investors should monitor competitive pressures and regulatory changes, but XP's disciplined execution and diversified ecosystem position it well for continued growth.
